workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Working on Blue Yonder Airlines's QBR preparation. Got the escalation email -- help me draft the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-25 14:06 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Blue Yonder Airlines QBR preparation

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-02-20 13:09 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Blue Yonder Airlines QBR preparation

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-02 11:43 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-G trap (Recommends commitments outside CSA authority); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-G failure mode (Recommends commitments outside CSA authority)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-G -- Recommends commitments outside CSA authority
